problems.text: Kenga always makes one large jump followed by two small jumps. Kenga starts at 0 and ends on 11. How many jumps did Kenga make?
A full glass of water weighs 400 g and the empty glass weighs 100 g. How many grams does a half-full glass of water weigh?

Albert rearranges the five number cards shown below to make the smallest possible 8-digit number. (He cannot cut a card to separate its digits — e.g. 59 is always 59, never 5 and 9.) Which card will be at the right-hand end?
problems.label: Card at the right end:

problems.text: How many squares will balance the three triangles?
3 hens lay 3 eggs in 3 days. How many eggs will 6 hens lay in 6 days?
